description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the microsoft/powershell Docker image for all jobs and runs a global before_script `apt-get -qq install git`. Define four stages: test, build, artifacts, and deploy. In the test stage add a `test` job that runs `pwsh -Command ". ./build.init.ps1; Invoke-Build Test"`. In the build stage add a `build` job that inherits the global before_script and then runs `pwsh -Command ". ./build.init.ps1; Invoke-Build Build"`, publishing the `.build` directory as an artifact that expires after 1 hour. In the artifacts stage add a `create_artifacts` job that first runs `chmod +x ./linuxbuilddependencies.sh` and `./linuxbuilddependencies.sh`, then executes `pwsh -Command ". ./build.init.ps1; Invoke-Build CreateArtifacts"`, and stores the `.build` directory as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a `pages` job that runs only on the `master` branch, runs `pwsh -Command ". ./build.init.ps1; Invoke-Build DeployDocs_GitLabPages"`, publishes the `public` directory as an artifact expiring after 1 week, and deploys to GitLab Pages at the specified URL.
